title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: The company is believed to be facing serious financial difficulties.
questions.afterGap: the company is facing serious financial difficulties.
questions.explanation: Tests the impersonal passive construction 'It is believed that'.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'll take you to the airport if you like,’ said my brother.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs with infinitive structures.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't go to the party, so I didn't meet her.
questions.beforeGap: If I had gone to the party, I
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for hypothetical past outcomes.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure it was a huge surprise to you when you won.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for certain past deduction.

questions.firstSentence: He had never before been so happy.
questions.explanation: Tests inversion with negative time expressions (At no time).

questions.firstSentence: It is much more difficult to learn Chinese than I expected.
questions.explanation: Tests negative comparison using 'not nearly as... as'.
